Title: Couple, son found dead in Sungai Petani house
Article:
SUNGAI PETANI: Three family members were found dead in their home at Kampung Belau, Merbok, here yesterday.
The victims, believed to be a couple aged 65 and 53, and their 29-year-old son, were found by police and villagers.
All three bodies were sent to Sultanah Bahiyah Hospital in Alor Star for post-mortem.
Kuala Muda police chief Assistant Commissioner Hanyan Ramlan is scheduled to hold a press conference regarding the case at the Merbok police station at noon today.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Government to intensify domestic drug production support under NIMP 2030: BMI
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The government will intensify support for domestic drug production to contain costs under the New Industrial Master Plan (NIMP 2030), according to research firm BMI.
In a statement today, the Fitch Solutions research unit said Malaysia aims to increase the share of domestically-produced medical products under NIMP 2030, creating a more supportive policy backdrop for the local manufacturing of essential medicines, including generic medicines.
"The Ministry of Health's (MOH) offtake policy to procure pharmaceutical products and critical medical devices will favour suppliers that invest in local production, while also strengthening the government's ability to renegotiate prices and prioritise lower-cost generic medicines where appropriate.
"Together, these measures will accelerate the shift in public procurement toward locally produced, cost-effective generic medicines," it said.
Meanwhile, BMI said in January 2026, the MOH announced that the government had saved more than RM900 million over the past two years by prioritising the use of generic medicines across both public and private healthcare.
It said central to this effort is a 'generic first' approach, under which a generic version, when available, is treated as the preferred prescribing option.
This was also highlighted by the 13th Malaysia Plan (2026-2030) and the Health White Paper, which prioritise healthcare access, reinforcing the role of generic medicines as the default option for broad-based access to essential therapies, said BMI.
Beyond lowering drug expenditure, the policy is designed to reshape clinicians' prescribing habits and strengthen public confidence in generic medicines, said BMI.
Under Budget 2026, total expenditure for public healthcare was set at RM419 billion, the first overall contraction since 2020. MOH still received RM46.5 billion, up from RM45.3 billion in 2025.
With supply security and cost containment highlighted as budget priorities, public-sector procurement is likely to shift further towards lower-cost generic medicines and biosimilars.
"Malaysia's National Generic Medicines Framework defines a generic medicine as a product intended to be interchangeable with the originator once patent or other exclusivity rights expire, enabling other manufacturers to produce and market equivalent therapies at lower cost.
"Together, these measures will accelerate the expansion of the generic medicine segment.  However, the government-driven shift towards more affordable treatment options will increase competitive pressure on innovative drugmakers looking to expand in Malaysia," it added.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: PKR's Abu Hafiz challenges Victor Chin to name MP in RM9.5mil bribe allegation
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: PKR Central Leadership Council member Dr Abu Hafiz Salleh Hudin has challenged businessman Victor Chin to "stop the drama" and publicly identify the member of parliament who allegedly extorted RM9.5 million from him.
He said that the party would not tolerate baseless allegations that tarnish its reputation or that of its members.
The sum was reportedly a "fee" paid to an unnamed government lawmaker to scrub Chin's reputation of the "corporate mafia" label, a moniker associated with his alleged involvement in takeovers and the use of aggressive, "thug-like" tactics to seize control of listed companies.
Abu Hafiz added that the claim remains mere hearsay as long as the perpetrator remains unnamed.
"We cannot accept a group of people who act with such impunity," he said, adding that transparency is vital for the integrity of the administration.
Since making his first high-profile appearance under the regulatory lens in 2025, the businessman has been the subject of an intensive probe by the police and the Securities Commission (SC).
On Tuesday, Chin alleged that a PKR MP asked for RM10 million in cash to settle the matter.
In a 40-page document outlining the alleged events, he said he initially prepared RM9.5 million.
He added that the remaining RM500,000 was not paid as the issue was not resolved.
The "corporate mafia" claims include allegations that senior MACC officers ran intimidation campaigns, pressuring executives and shareholders of public-listed firms to resign or sell shares at steep discounts to connected businessmen.
On March 16, police
raided
several of Chin's premises in Kemensah and Sungai Long, Kajang.
Yesterday, PKR vice-president Datuk Seri R. Ramanan
denied
allegations linking him to Chin and the alleged corporate mafia.
The Sungai Buloh member of parliament challenged Chin to substantiate his accusations and not keep the public guessing over the identity of "Mr R", describing the claims as baseless and part of a broader attempt to discredit the government.
Former economy minister Datuk Seri Rafizi Ramli also denied allegations linking him to the bribes.
The Pandan MP said he could directly name "Mr R" based on the information provided by Chin, but he added that the bigger issue at present is the businessman's allegation that RM9.5 million was paid to a PKR MP.
He added that he will reveal the identity of "Mr R" today.
Chin is currently being investigated under the Anti-Money Laundering, Anti-Terrorism Financing and Proceeds of Unlawful Activities Act (Amla).
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Dark-coloured waste, vandalism force closure of Johor Baru water park
Article:
JOHOR BARU: The water park at Hutan Bandar has been temporarily closed for repairs following acts of vandalism, the Johor Baru City Council (MBJB) said.
In a post on its official Facebook page, the council said the closure was necessary to ensure public safety and to allow maintenance work on affected facilities.
It added that irresponsible behaviour by some individuals had affected cleanliness and damaged public amenities, causing inconvenience to other visitors.
Photos shared by the council showed dark-coloured waste left along the tiled walkway near the pool area.
MBJB said the water park is a shared public facility that must be used responsibly.
Visitors were urged to follow regulations, maintain cleanliness and take care of public property.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Three cops hurt in scuffle at George Town flats
Article:
GEORGE TOWN: Three policemen ended up with minor injuries after they were involved in a scuffle with several individuals during an enforcement operation at a flat in Jalan Sungai here last night.
Northeast district police chief Assistant Commissioner Abdul Rozak Muhammad said the incident happened after the policemen rushed to the area following a request for assistance from personnel on the ground who encountered a hostile crowd while pursuing a suspect.
He said four policemen had been pursuing a motorcyclist who fled towards the flats area before a misunderstanding occurred with several residents when one of the policeman's motorcycle skidded and rammed into a food stall.
Abdul Rozak said the case is being investigated under Section 147 of the Penal Code for rioting and Section 353 of the same law for using criminal force or intimidation to deter a public servant from discharging his duties.
He said eight men, aged between 28 and 38, had been arrested in connection with the incident.
All of them have been remanded for three days.
Several video footages of the incident have since gone viral since last night.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: HSBC sees Malaysia's economy staying on solid growth path in 2026
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: HSBC Private Bank expects Malaysia's economy to maintain solid growth in 2026, despite short-term risks from global uncertainties.
HSBC Private Bank and Premier Wealth investment strategist Abhilash Narayan said strong domestic consumption, continued infrastructure and data centre investment, and a recovery in tourism continue to be supportive for the country's economic growth this year.
In its second-quarter (Q2) 2026 investment outlook, HSBC Private Bank forecasts Malaysia's real gross domestic product to grow 4.5 per cent this year, slightly moderating from 4.9 per cent in 2025.
"Elevated energy prices and Middle East conflict present near-term headwinds. However, Malaysia's policy responses, including targeted subsidies and pricing controls, cushion economic impacts.
"Policy flexibility and medium-term regional growth prospects remain constructive," Abhilash said in a statement.
He added that inflation has been gradually rising due to strong domestic demand, but limited wage pressures are expected to keep price growth manageable.
He said Bank Negara Malaysia is projected to maintain interest rates at 2.75 per cent through 2026 unless oil prices spike significantly.
On the ringgit, Abhilash said the currency has been one of Asia's best-performing currencies against the US dollar this year, bolstered by corporate foreign exchange repatriations, a current account surplus and a robust pipeline of foreign direct investment.
"However, as a result of the recent outperformance, the ringgit is somewhat expensive relative to fundamentals.
"Hence, we hold a neutral view on the currency over the next six months as the pace of appreciation could slow down. We expect the ringgit to edge towards 3.85 against the US dollar by end-2026," he said.
Abhilash added that Malaysian equities are trading at a 14.8 times forward price-to-earnings ratio, in line with the five-year average. He said corporate earnings could rise nearly nine per cent in 2026.
"We are neutral on Malaysian equities over the next six months despite the strong economic outlook, as growth areas such as electronics, chips and data centres have limited representation in the stock index," he said.
HSBC Private Bank's Q2 2026 investment outlook, Changing Narratives, Continued Opportunity, underscores that the resilient global economy continues to create growth opportunities, allowing investors to remain invested while strengthening portfolio resilience amid geopolitical developments.
The bank advises private wealth clients to focus on broad diversification rather than market timing, noting that historical market reactions to geopolitical events are usually temporary.
While the report maintains a global preference for growth-style stocks and companies with pricing power, it also highlights Asia as a key source of long-term growth and resilient income opportunities.
Selective allocations to hedge funds, private markets, bonds, currencies, commodities and multi-asset strategies are recommended to manage concentration risk and enhance stability.
Global chief investment officer Willem Sels said investors should focus on building resilient portfolios rather than chasing short-term market narratives.
"The year-to-date volatility of gold has demonstrated that no single diversifier is perfect, so we continue to diversify our diversifiers across sectors, geographies and asset classes," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Golden Destinations targets RM90mil in IPO on ACE Market
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Golden Destinations Group Bhd aims to raise RM90 million through its initial public offering (IPO) as part of its listing on the Ace Market.
The IPO is priced at 45 sen a share, giving the company an estimated market capitalisation of RM450 million upon listing.
The expected listing for the company is on April 16.
More than half of the proceeds or RM50 million will be used for the company to set up a new centralised headquarters over the next three years.
The remainder is earmarked for promotional activities, expansion in Sarawak and Singapore, capital expenditure on IT system and infrastructure as well as expansion of workforce.
Other expenses for the amount raised include working capital and listing expenses.
Golden Destinations specialises in curating travel experience packages under its Golden Destinations flagship brand portfolio. It operates primarily within the B2B segment, serving a network of travel agents.
Under the B2B model, Golden Destinations typically does not sell outbound travel experience products and services directly to the end-consumers.
Its IPO comprises the issuance of 200 million new shares and 100 million offer for sale of existing shares.
Of the total shares offered, 50 million will be made available to the Malaysian public, including
25 million issue shares earmarked for Bumiputera investors under the balloting process.
A further 35 million shares will be reserved for eligible directors and employees of the group, as well as individuals who have contributed to the group's success.
The offer for sale comprises 100 million shares to be placed out to identified Bumiputera investors approved by the Investment, Trade and Industry Ministry.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Couple jailed one year for indecent act at Penang cemetery
Article:
GEORGE TOWN: A couple, arrested for an indecent act at a Chinese cemetery here, was sentenced to one year's jail by the magistrate's court here today after they pleaded guilty to the offence.
Magistrate Nadratun Naim Mohd Saidi handed down the sentence on M. Jegathesan, 58, and Halila Abu Bakar, 37, after they admitted to the charge under Section 377D of the Penal Code.
Both admitted to jointly committing an act of gross indecency at a Chinese cemetery in Batu Gantung, Ayer Itam at  8.30am on March 22.
The charge was framed under Section 377D of the Penal Code, read together with Section 34 of the same code, which provides for punishment under the same provision.
The provision criminalises acts of "gross indecency" between persons, whether in public or private, and carries a penalty of up to two years' imprisonment.
MORE TO COME
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: MTT Shipping and Logistics prices IPO at RM1.03 per share, raising RM652.5mil
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: MTT Shipping and Logistics Bhd (MTTSL) is set to raise up to RM652.5 million with its debut on the Main Market of Bursa Malaysia scheduled for April 21.
The company has fixed its initial public offering (IPO) price at RM1.03 per share, according to its prospectus launched today.
The IPO comprises 633.5 million issue shares, with 312.5 million shares allocated for Bumiputera investors approved by the Ministry of Investment, Trade and Industry (MITI), 258.5 million shares for Malaysian and foreign institutional investors, and 12.5 million shares reserved for the company directors, employees, and key contributors.
The remaining 50 million shares will be reserved for the Malaysian public via balloting, split evenly between Bumiputera and non-Bumiputera.
According to the prospectus, RM624.7 million of the raised proceeds, equivalent to 95.7 per cent of the total amount, will be utilised for the acquisition of at least 12 container vessels within 3 years of the listing date.
The remaining 4.3 per cent, or RM27.8 million, will be allocated for the reimbursement of listing expenses within the first 6 months upon listing.
MTTSL aims to strategically widen and extend its footprint within and beyond the Southeast Asian region, allowing the team to capitalise on the growing demand expected for domestic and regional trade.
The group will also be implementing a policy that targets a dividend payout of at least 50 per cent of its profit after tax.
Upon full subscription, Malaysia's leading logistics company would be valued at RM652.5 million, placing its total market capitalisation at RM2.6 billion.
Applications for the retail tranche will close on April 3.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Open fires dominate emergency lines, accounting for up to 60pct of calls
Article:
PETALING JAYA: Up to 60 per cent of emergency calls received by the fire and rescue department during the hot season are linked to open fires, including bush, forest and plantation blazes, authorities say.
Fire and Rescue Department Director-general Datuk Seri Ts. Nor Hisham Mohammad said fire incidents involving forests, plantations and bushland remain on a worrying upward trend, with 32,810 cases recorded between 2024 and February this year.
He said 2024 saw the highest number of cases at 16,294, followed by 9,941 in 2025 and 6,575 in the first two months of 2026.
"Bush and grass fires remain the largest contributor, with 26,800 cases, followed by forest fires (3,860) and farm and plantation fires (2,150)," he told Kosmo!.
He said authorities are paying close attention to high-risk "hotspot" areas, including peatlands and forest reserves in Selangor, Perak and Johor.
Fires in peatland are particularly dangerous as they can burn underground, making them difficult to detect and potentially allowing them to spread for weeks if not contained early.
In response, the fire department is stepping up the use of technology, including drones, for aerial monitoring to identify hotspots before fires escalate.
Nor Hisham urged the public to report any signs of smoke or small fires via the emergency hotline 999.
He also called on the public to act as the authorities' "eyes and ears" by reporting suspected open burning to the Department of Environment hotline at 1-800-88-2727.
Those found carrying out open burning may face fines of up to RM500,000, imprisonment of up to five years, or both, under the Environmental Quality Act 1974.
Kosmo! reported that an oil palm plantation spanning about 60.7 hectares in Kampung Jemeri, Kuala Rompin, and bushland covering roughly 150 hectares along both sides of the Punggai Bypass towards Sebana Cove in Kota Tinggi were recently hit by fires.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: MARKET PULSE AM MAR 26, 2026 [WATCH]
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Bursa Malaysia's 30-stock index opened higher today but later pared gains, despite Wall Street closing on a positive note overnight.
Analysts anticipate some bargain hunting could support the market as global sentiment turns slightly bullish amid easing US-Iran tensions.
The index is expected to trade within the 1,710–1,720 range for the day.
In the cryptocurrency market, Bitcoin climbed to RM283,498.
Meanwhile, Ethereum rose to RM8,624 and Solana stood at RM365.
That's it for Market Pulse.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Fuel supply at petrol stations nationwide remains sufficient, says petrol dealers group
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Fuel supply at petrol stations nationwide remains sufficient, the Petrol Dealers Association of Malaysia (PDAM) said.
Public concerns over shortages have grown amid current global market uncertainties, with reports of supply disruptions and long queues in other countries.
PDAM confirmed that operational conditions at Malaysian petrol stations are stable, with supply deliveries proceeding smoothly and without technical disruptions.
"Although the global market is challenging, Malaysia has a mature supply chain," the group told Berita Harian.
"Through close cooperation between the government and oil companies, domestic supply remains secure and is not at risk of shortages like those seen elsewhere."
PDAM said it continues to play a key role in ensuring petrol station stock levels are sufficient and well-managed.
Measures include strict guidelines to prevent leakages and excessive purchasing, while RON95 supply is prioritised for locally registered vehicles. Operators are also reminded to monitor stock levels to ensure fair access for all consumers.
In Bayan Lepas, fuel purchases exceeding 20 litres into containers require a controlled goods permit from the Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Ministry.
Penang Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Ministry director S Jegan said permits are issued for specific purposes, such as operating agricultural or fisheries machinery, and are subject to conditions.
"The ministry continuously monitors petrol stations. Fuel sales into containers exceeding 20 litres without a valid permit are an offence under the Supply Control Act," he said.
This follows viral images and dashcam footage showing an individual filling fuel into containers at a local station.
Jegan confirmed the person had a valid permit in line with legal provisions.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: French court finds Swiss Islamic scholar Ramadan guilty of rape
Article:
PARIS: Swiss Islamic scholar Tariq Ramadan was sentenced on Wednesday to 18 years in prison by a Paris criminal court for the rape of three women.
The verdict marks the latest fall from grace for the former Oxford University professor, who faces a string of rape allegations in Switzerland and France and has already been convicted in the former.
A charismatic yet controversial figure in European Islam, Ramadan has always maintained his innocence.
While he had been on trial in Paris since March 2 for rapes allegedly committed against three women between 2009 and 2016, Ramadan did not appear in court in person.
His lawyer cited a "flare-up" of multiple sclerosis requiring hospitalisation in Geneva to explain his absence – but this was rejected by a court-ordered medical assessment.
Presiding Judge Corinne Goetzmann said a warrant for the 63-year-old's arrest had been issued. The court also barred the scholar from setting foot on French territory again after the end of his sentence.
In 2025, Switzerland's supreme court rejected Ramadan's appeal against his conviction for the rape of a woman in a Geneva hotel nearly two decades ago.
It upheld the sentence of three years in prison, two of which were suspended, handed down by the court in Geneva.
Before word of his crimes came to light, Ramadan was a professor of contemporary Islamic studies at Oxford and held visiting roles at universities in Qatar and Morocco.
He was forced to take a leave of absence in 2017 when rape allegations surfaced in France at the height of the "Me Too" movement. - AFP
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Takiyuddin tells MACC, police to probe RM10mil bribe claims involving PKR MP [WATCH]
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Perikatan Nasional (PN) secretary-general Datuk Seri Takiyuddin Hassan has called on the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) and police to probe claims that a PKR member of parliament received nearly RM10 million in bribes.
Takiyuddin also called on the government, particularly PKR leaders, to explain the allegations and fully cooperate with investigators.
"Failure to address this openly and with integrity will erode public confidence in the government's fight against corruption and abuse of power," he said.
On Tuesday, businessman Victor Chin, also known as Chin Boon Loong and linked to a "corporate mafia" scandal, alleged that a PKR MP asked for RM10 million in cash to settle the matter.
In a 40-page document outlining the alleged events, he said he initially prepared RM9.5 million.
He added that the remaining RM500,000 was not paid as the issue was not resolved.
The "corporate mafia" claims include allegations that senior MACC officers ran intimidation campaigns, pressuring executives and shareholders of public-listed firms to resign or sell shares at steep discounts to connected businessmen.
On March 16, police raided several of Chin's premises in Kemensah and Sungai Long, Kajang.
LINK:
https://www.nst.com.my/news/nation/2026/03/1398850/corporate-mafia-probe-why-raid-my-house-asks-victor-chin
Yesterday, PKR vice-president Datuk Seri R. Ramanan denied allegations linking him to Chin and the alleged corporate mafia.
The Sungai Buloh member of parliament challenged Chin to substantiate his accusations and not keep the public guessing over the identity of "Mr R", describing the claims as baseless and part of a broader attempt to discredit the government.
LINK:
https://www.nst.com.my/news/nation/2026/03/1403523/ramanan-denies-he-mr-r-challenges-victor-chin-name-him
Former economy minister Datuk Seri Rafizi Ramli also denied allegations linking him to the bribes.
The Pandan MP said he could directly name "Mr R" based on the information provided by Chin, but he added that the bigger issue at present is the businessman's allegation that RM9.5 million was paid to a PKR MP.
He added that he will reveal the identity of "Mr R" today.
LINK:
https://www.nst.com.my/amp/news/nation/2026/03/1403462/it-wasnt-me-rafizi-denies-rm95m-claim
Takiyuddin said the identity of the MP should only be determined through due process to avoid speculation that could harm innocent parties.
He said the allegations were serious, involving a large sum and raising concerns over corporate governance, political influence and institutional integrity.
The Kota Baru MP said more troubling were claims that the money was meant to secure political interference in corporate matters.
"If true, this is a serious breach of good governance and integrity long championed by the government.
"It also risks creating the perception that political influence is being used to interfere in corporate affairs and law enforcement," he said.
He added that PN would monitor the case and raise it in Parliament if necessary to uphold accountability and integrity.
"Integrity is not just rhetoric. It must be proven through consistent transparency and accountability," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Couple arrive in Penang court to face indecency charges [WATCH]
Article:
GEORGE TOWN: A couple arrested for an indecent act at a local Chinese cemetery arrived at the magistrate's court to face charges.
They appeared at about 8.15am, escorted by police and handcuffed.
They are expected to face charges under Section 377D of the Penal Code.
The law criminalises acts of "gross indecency" between persons, in public or private, with a maximum penalty of two years' imprisonment.
Police launched an investigation after a 69-year-old man reported a video showing a man and woman behaving inappropriately at the cemetery.
The 38-second video had gone viral, showing a man standing naked with his back to the camera, while a partially undressed woman sits on a tombstone.
The woman filming confronts them, saying they have been spotted at the cemetery before.
In the clip, the man is seen putting on his trousers.
The woman filming reprimands them, stressing that a cemetery is meant to honor the deceased.
Although the
man apologises,
the woman dismisses it and warns that the matter will be reported to the police.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: US jury finds Meta, YouTube liable in social media addiction trial
Article:
LOS ANGELES: A Los Angeles jury on Wednesday found Meta and YouTube liable for harming a young woman because of an addictive design of their social media platforms, ordering the companies to pay $6 million in damages, including $3 million in punitive damages.
The verdict hands plaintiffs in more than a thousand similar pending cases significant leverage – and signals to the broader tech industry that juries are prepared to hold social media companies accountable for the mental health toll of their design choices.
The jury answered yes to all seven questions on verdict forms for both companies, finding that Meta and YouTube were negligent in the design and operation of their platforms and that their negligence was a substantial factor in causing harm to the plaintiff.
Jurors also found that both companies knew or should have known their services posed a danger to minors, that they failed to adequately warn users of that danger, and that a reasonable platform operator would have done so.
The panel awarded $3 million in compensatory damages, assigning Meta 70 percent of the responsibility for the plaintiff's harm – a $2.1 million share – and YouTube the remaining 30 percent, or $900,000.
In a second phase, jurors added a further $3 million in total punitive damages after finding both companies had acted with malice, oppression or fraud.
Both companies said they would appeal the verdict.
"This case misunderstands YouTube, which is a responsibly built streaming platform, not a social media site," Google spokesperson Jose Castaneda said.
A spokesperson for Meta said they "respectfully disagree with the verdict," adding that "teen mental health is profoundly complex and cannot be linked to a single app."
Nine of the 12 furors further found that both companies had acted with malice, oppression or fraud, a finding that set the stage for the separate punitive damages.
The plaintiff, known in court documents by her initials K.G.M. and called Kaley at trial, began using YouTube when she was six, downloading the app on her iPod Touch to watch videos about lip gloss and an online kids game.
She joined Instagram at nine, getting around a block her mother had put in place to keep her off the platform.
She told jurors that her near-constant social media use "really affected my self-worth," saying the apps led her to abandon hobbies, struggle to make friends and constantly measure herself against others.
In closing arguments, plaintiff attorney Mark Lanier cast the case as a story of corporate greed, saying that features like infinite scrolling, autoplay videos, notifications and like counts were engineered to drive compulsive use among young people.
- 'Existential' threat? -
Meta and YouTube had maintained throughout the trial that Kaley's mental health struggles had nothing to do with their platforms.
Meta's lawyer pointed to her life at home and a turbulent relationship with her parents, while YouTube disputed how much time Kaley actually spent on its platform.
The jury rejected both defences across all seven questions on each verdict form.
TikTok and Snap were originally named as defendants but settled on undisclosed terms before the trial got underway.
Two further bellwether trials are expected to follow in the same Los Angeles courthouse, with their outcomes likely to determine whether social media companies fight on or move toward a broader settlement, potentially including redesigning how their platforms work.
The penalty amounts are "a slap on the wrist for companies like Meta and YouTube, which are two of the biggest ad sellers in the world," said Jasmine Enberg of Scalable, who tracks the social media industry.
"But if these companies are forced to redesign their products, that poses an existential threat to their business models."
A separate New Mexico jury on Tuesday found Meta liable for endangering children by making them vulnerable to predators on its platforms and other dangers.
The state had sought the maximum $2.2 billion in damages, but the jury awarded a lesser amount of $375 million. - AFP
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Stocks on edge as Middle East ceasefire talks take centre stage
Article:
SINGAPORE: Asian stocks struggled for direction while the dollar held firm on Thursday as investors treaded cautiously amid dizzying developments in the Middle East, where Iran said it would weigh a US proposal to end the Gulf conflict.
The widening war has jolted global markets, sending oil prices soaring, reigniting inflation fears and scrambling global rate expectations.
It was a mixed picture in Asia in early trading with Japan's Nikkei up 0.6 per cent while South Korean stocks were down one per cent. MSCI's broadest index of Asia-Pacific shares outside Japan edged 0.23 per cent lower, set for an 8.7 per cent decline in the month, its biggest monthly drop since October 2022.
The dollar held firm near recent highs and was on track for a two per cent monthly gain, cementing its status as the markets' preferred safe haven.
The latest comments by Iran suggested some willingness by Tehran to negotiate an end to the war if its demands were met. The US sent a 15-point ceasefire proposal to Iran that was originally brushed aside by Iranian officials.
"While the headline flow points to a more constructive tone, markets remain unsure which signals to trust and act upon," Chris Weston, head of research at Pepperstone, said.
"Price action suggests participants expect further twists and turns, even as the probability of a negotiated outcome edges higher."
The near month-long war triggered by joint US–Israeli strikes on Iran in late February has effectively shut the Strait of Hormuz, a conduit for a fifth of global oil and liquefied natural gas flows.
The disruption has sent prices surging above US$100 per barrel. Brent crude futures were at US$103.35 per barrel, up one per cent on the day, and set for a 42 per cent jump in the month.
"If you look at what the US wants to achieve, what Israel wants to achieve, and what Tehran wants to achieve, it will be very hard to reconcile all these points," said Matthias Scheiber, senior portfolio manager and the head of the Multi Asset team at Allspring Global Investments.
"We still think there is a case to make for structurally higher energy prices for the moment."
Fears of an inflationary aftershock from soaring energy prices have pushed traders to price out any chance of a Federal Reserve rate cut this year, lifting the dollar. Bets on US rate hikes briefly gained traction but have since been pared back.
European Central Bank President Christine Lagarde opened the door on Wednesday to raising interest rates in the euro zone if war in the Middle East pushes up inflation in the region for some time.
"If the shock gives rise to a large though not-too-persistent overshoot of our target, some measured adjustment of policy could be warranted," Lagarde said in Frankfurt.
The euro was little changed at US$1.1562, while sterling bought US$1.3358. The yen hovered at 159.43 per dollar, clinging to the closely watched 160 level that traders see as a potential trigger for intervention.
In commodities, gold was 0.66 per cent higher at US$4,537 per ounce, but has largely sold off this month and is on course for a 14 per cent drop in the month, its steepest fall since October 2008.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Retiree collects thousands of discarded lighters to decorate home
Article:
KUALA TERENGGANU: A 71-year-old, Ismail Hassan, has turned his Kampung Kuala Bekah home into a vibrant, plastic-paved landmark through his unique approach to recycling.
While others sort their waste into bins, the retiree has spent the last three years adorning his walls with nearly 4,000 discarded lighters.
What began as a dormant curiosity has grown into a full-blown retirement passion.
"I have always had an affinity for collecting them, but I didn't have the time when I was younger," he said.
Ismail cycles daily to local wakaf (pavilions) and public resting spots, where spent lighters are often discarded.
His collection has also grown through the generosity of friends who now regularly contribute their own spent lighters instead of throwing them away.
[[nid:1403892]]
The spark for this unconventional project ignited during a visit to Setiu, where Ismail encountered a house decorated with recycled glass bottles.
"I thought, why not lighters? They are colourful, easy to source, and frequently thrown away as rubbish," he said.
His walls are neatly arranged with the lighters.
Using wire mesh and transparent fishing line, he aligns each row perfectly.
"Beyond just a hobby, there is immense satisfaction in taking something discarded and transforming it into a conversation piece that people find beautiful," he added.
With parts of his wall still bare, Ismail continues his mission, one bicycle ride at a time.
